title: AMD's upcoming Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 listed around $1,000 at several retailers across Canada and the UK New flagship dual-cache CPU might demand a hefty premium
summary: AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 CPU listings have surfaced with preliminary prices. The dual-cache processor is expected to cost around $1,000 at various retailers, a significant premium over the standard 9950X3D. This new flagship model may come with a hefty price tag.
insight: Ryzen 9 9950X3D2's price may be too steep for consumers

title: UK confirms drone-killing DragonFire laser weapon for Royal Navy destroyers by 2027 laser downs 400mph high speed drones, costs $13 per shot
summary: The UK Ministry of Defence confirms DragonFire laser installation on Royal Navy Type 45 destroyers by 2027, five years ahead of schedule. The DragonFire high-energy laser weapon can down 400mph high-speed drones at a cost of $13 per shot.
insight: Enhanced maritime defense capabilities against high-speed drone threats.

title: Get a 27" 1440p OLED monitor with a blazing-fast 240 Hz refresh rate for just $499 LG's 27GS93QE-B is $400 off right now, features 1,300 nits of peak HDR brightness
summary: The LG 27GS93QE-B is a 27-inch 1440p OLED monitor with a 240 Hz refresh rate, ideal for gaming, productivity, and media consumption.
insight: Blazing-fast OLED gaming monitor at an affordable price point.

title: Iran threatens complete and utter annihilation of OpenAI's $30B Stargate AI data center in Abu Dhabi regime posts video with satellite imagery of ChatGPT-maker's premier 1GW data center
summary: Iran's IRGC threatens annihilation of OpenAI's $30B Stargate AI data center in Abu Dhabi.
insight: Global AI infrastructure vulnerable to geopolitics and military threats.

title: Researchers train living rat neurons to perform real-time AI computations experiments could pave the way for new brain-machine interfaces
summary: Japanese researchers trained rat neurons to generate complex signals using AI, paving the way for brain-machine interfaces.
insight: Neurons can autonomously perform AI computations in real-time.

title: Intel's upcoming Wildcat Lake low-budget CPUs leak out again OEM confirms specs for Core 7 350, Core 5 320, & Core 3 305 in first retail product datasheet
summary: Intel's Wildcat Lake CPU family has leaked in a new OEM datasheet, confirming specs for three SKUs. The datasheet, from Advantech, lists the Core 7 350, Core 5 320, and Core 3 305 CPUs. This suggests that the launch of Wildcat Lake is imminent.
insight: New retail datasheet confirms specs for Intel's Wildcat Lake CPUs

title: Delisted Xbox 360 games briefly reappear on the Xbox Store Microsoft prepares backward compatibility revival
summary: Delisted Xbox 360 games briefly reappear on Xbox Store, hinting at a backward compatibility revival. This move is seen as a response to Xbox Vice President Jason Ronald's commitment at GDC 2026. The revival aims to increase the availability of classic games on modern platforms.
insight: Microsoft restores nostalgic gaming options for loyal fan base

title: Steam starts gathering FPS data with latest client update company to estimate framerates based on your hardware, Beta feature to focus on SteamOS devices
summary: Steam's latest update includes a Beta feature to gather FPS data, enabling the platform to monitor and compare framerates with your hardware. This feature is currently only available on SteamOS devices. It aims to provide more accurate hardware estimations.
insight: Improved hardware estimations through real-world FPS data collection.

title: Autonomous ErgoChair Core Review: You get what you pay for
summary: The Autonomous ErgoChair Core is an entry-level office chair with limited dimensions. It offers some comfort features but may not meet expectations for a mid-range price point. Users should weigh its value based on their specific needs and budget.
insight: You get what you pay for with this affordable office chair.

title: Nvidia Pascal GPUs debuted 10 years ago today, best known for the GTX 1060 and GTX 1080 Ti architecture kicked off with the Tesla P100
summary: Nvidia's Tesla P100 launched 10 years ago, marking five architectural breakthroughs. The Nvidia Pascal GPUs, including the GTX 1060 and GTX 1080 Ti, followed suit. This launch paved the way for future Nvidia innovations.
insight: Nvidia's Pascal architecture revolutionized gaming and AI computing performance.

title: BenQ DesignVue PD2770U 4K professional monitor review: A blend of flexibility, control, and precision
summary: The BenQ DesignVue PD2770U monitor offers a 27-inch 4K IPS panel with wide-gamut color, HDR10, and a built-in calibrator for professional-grade visuals.
insight: Precision and control for creatives and professionals at fingertips.

title: Hobbyist builds a homebrew Intel 8086 ISA accelerator card maker s project improves integer multiplication on these retro systems by 250%
summary: A computer scientist created a hardware ISA card accelerator for Intel 8086 and 8088 systems, significantly improving integer multiplication performance. This project showcases a creative approach to enhancing retro computing capabilities. The accelerator boosts integer multiplication by 250%.
insight: Revitalizing retro systems with significant performance boosts through innovative hardware upgrades.

title: Intel's upcoming 42-core Nova Lake SKU allegedly upgraded to 44 cores New config frees up 6P+12E tiles that could trickle down as locked bLLC variants
summary: Intel's 42-core Nova Lake SKU has been upgraded to 44 cores, achieving symmetry across its dual-tile configuration. This upgrade frees up 6P+12E tiles that could potentially be used as locked variants with increased bLLC capacity. The 6P+12E tiles may become a new 22-core SKU.
insight: Increased core count and bLLC capacity enhance processor performance and efficiency.

title: PlayStation 3 emulator makes Cell CPU 'breakthrough' that improves performance in all games 'All CPUs can benefit from this, from low-end to high-end!' says RPCS3 devs
summary: RPCS3 developers claim a breakthrough in emulating the PS3's Cell Broadband Engine processor, which improves performance in all games. This advancement has the potential to benefit all CPUs, from low-end to high-end. The open-source emulator is now able to run games more efficiently.
insight: Advancements in emulating the PS3's Cell CPU benefit all CPU types.

title: $1,700 liquid-cooled phone can run GTA V at up to 100 FPS, Red Dead 2 at 50+ FPS via emulation Redmagic 11 Pro packs 24 GB of RAM and pulls more than 40W at peak load
summary: The Redmagic 11 Pro offers powerful performance with liquid cooling, allowing it to run demanding games like GTA V at up to 100 FPS and Red Dead 2 at 50+ FPS via emulation. This is made possible by its 24 GB of RAM and robust cooling system. The phone's high price of $1,700 is justified by its gaming capabilities.
insight: High-end gaming performance in a handheld device is now affordable

title: Apple approves drivers that let AMD and Nvidia eGPUs run on Mac software designed for AI, though, and not built for gaming
summary: Apple has approved drivers for AMD and Nvidia eGPUs to run on Macs, making them legitimate software for Macs. The approved drivers allow the eGPUs to work with Mac software designed for AI, not gaming. This development eliminates the need for workarounds.
insight: Advancements in AI on Macs with approved eGPU drivers accelerate innovation

title: Player defeats Darks Souls II using only poop 42 hits of dung pie defeats the final boss
summary: Gamer ymfah used dung pies to defeat Dark Souls II, exploiting a mechanic that allows for creative solutions. This involved farming dung pies and perfecting timing to overcome challenges. The accomplishment showcases the game's depth and potential for innovative play.
insight: Creativity and resourcefulness can lead to innovative gaming solutions.

title: $2 WeAct CH32V006F8U6 Mini Core board features CH32V006 RISC-V MCU, supports 3.3V or 5V I/O voltage
summary: The WeAct CH32V006F8U6 Mini Core board is a tiny, inexpensive development board featuring the CH32V006 RISC-V microcontroller. It supports 3.3V or 5V I/O voltage and comes equipped with a USB-C port and two rows of 12-pin headers for I/O expansion.
insight: Inexpensive RISC-V boards expand MCU options for makers and developers

title: Advantech MIO-5356 An industrial 3.5-inch SBC based on Intel Core Series 3 Wildcat Lake SoC
summary: The Advantech MIO-5356 is a 3.5-inch SBC powered by Intel Core Series 3 Wildcat Lake SoC, supporting up to 64GB DDR5 memory and multiple connectivity options for industrial applications.
insight: High-performance computing for industrial applications with advanced connectivity options.

title: This Android phone can run Windows games locally, no PC required
summary: Red Magic's Android phone can run Windows games directly on its OS, eliminating the need for a separate PC.
insight: Native game compatibility enhances mobile gaming experience significantly

title: Nvidia shows neural compression can cut VRAM usage from 6.5GB to 970MB
summary: Nvidia's Neural Texture Compression (NTC) technology reduces VRAM usage from 6.5GB to 970MB, maintaining image quality.
insight: Significant VRAM reduction for improved gaming performance and lower system requirements.

title: A new OnlyOffice fork is Europe's answer to Microsoft Office
summary: Euro-Office, a new open-source office suite, is developed by Nextcloud and others, offering Microsoft-compatible formats and open standards support.
insight: European alternative to Microsoft Office emerges with open-source roots.

title: Sony quietly removes PC mentions from PlayStation Studios pages
summary: Sony has updated the PlayStation Studios' main banner with new game titles, removing Demon's Souls Remake from the lineup.
insight: Sony subtly shifts focus from PC gaming to console exclusives

title: Italian court says Netflix must refund customers up to $576 over price hikes
summary: An Italian court has ruled that Netflix must refund customers following price hikes imposed by Netflix Italia. The refund amount could be up to $576 per customer. The decision was made in response to an injunction action by consumer group Movimento Consumatori.
insight: Consumer protection laws hold powerful streaming services accountable for price changes.
